human_verification.notes: Reviewed Issue #1013 requirements for ad-operations-specific workflow handling, evidence and confidentiality expectations, advisory, editorial, legal, contractual, and signoff boundaries, scenario completeness, and validated packaging consistency.

review_notes: All required abilities across shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and professional-and-information-services sets are declared and validated.

Scenario coverage includes normal ad operations execution, incomplete inputs, QA failures, missing completion evidence, confidentiality conflicts, delivery exceptions, adjacent-lane routing, low-confidence facts, conflicting policies, realistic terminology, and tenant adaptation.
Package remains specification-based and documents a separate promotion path for any future retained-implementation runtime.

specialist_owned_evidence: Outputs are bounded to evidence-backed campaign workflow status, trafficking readiness, creative and tag QA, launch proof, delivery exceptions, confidentiality handling, and completion evidence rather than legal, editorial, privacy, media strategy, financial, contractual, or final signoff authority.

Source set includes IAB/4A's media-buy terms, IAB Tech Lab OpenRTB, VAST, and ads.txt standards, MRC measurement guidance, FTC online advertising guidance, and NAI self-regulatory privacy framework with explicit refresh and decay posture.

Boundary logic explicitly prevents unsupported launch, unauthorized disclosure, unreviewed claim or creative approval, unresolved delivery discrepancy, and generic treatment of materially different ad channels or platform workflows.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; non-blocking ambiguity is recorded as tenant, channel, publisher, platform, privacy, and authority prerequisites for specialized ad operations execution.
Runtime remains specification-based; retained-implementation promotion requires separate governance approval, telemetry evidence, retrieval wiring, mutation safeguards, and non-regression validation.
